---
title: "OSMnx"
---

::: {.lead}
The OSMnx tab downloads and analyzes real-world street networks from
OpenStreetMap. It exposes the full OSMnx toolbox — download, projection,
routing, accessibility, statistics, features, and elevation/terrain — adapted to
the Blender environment.
:::

The root **OSMnx** panel shows the active network's name, node/edge counts,
place, and CRS once a graph is imported. Most subpanels require an imported
OSMnx graph and display a hint pointing to **Download** until one exists.
Subpanels appear in workflow order.

## Download {#download}

Imports a street network into Blender.

| Method | Inputs |
| --- | --- |
| **Place** | A place name geocoded by Nominatim (e.g. `Madrid, Spain`); a geocoder result index disambiguates results. |
| **Multi-Place** | Several place names (one per line), unioned into a single graph. |
| **Point** | Latitude/longitude centre plus a radius in metres. |
| **Address** | A postal address plus a search radius. |
| **BBox** | North/south/east/west bounding-box coordinates. |
| **Polygon** | A mesh object whose vertices are treated as `(lon, lat)`. |
| **XML** | A local `.osm` XML file. |

**Network Type & Filter** — choose a `network_type` (drive, walk, bike, etc.),
a custom-filter preset, or an advanced filter string. **Options** include
**Simplify**, **Keep Curved Geometry**, **Truncate by Edge**, **Retain All**,
and a **Scale Factor**. **Import from OSM** runs the download.

## Graph Operations {#graph-ops}

Transform the imported graph.

- **Projection** — project the WGS84 graph to a metric CRS (**Project to CRS**);
  the current CRS is shown when projected.
- **Graph Type Conversion** — convert **To Undirected** or **To DiGraph**.
- **GeoDataFrame Conversion** — round-trip **Graph to GeoDataFrames** and back.
- **Simplification** — **Simplify Graph** and **Consolidate Intersections**
  (with a tolerance in metres).
- **Truncation** — truncate to a bounding box, polygon, or distance, and **Keep
  Largest Component**.

## Network Attributes {#attributes}

Compute edge metrics. Checkmarks indicate metrics already present.

- **Lengths** — add edge lengths.
- **Bearings** — add edge bearings (reports the bearing entropy).
- **Speed & Travel Time** — add edge speeds (with a fallback speed in km/h) and
  travel times. Travel times require speeds first.

## Routing & Paths {#routing}

- **Source & Target Nodes** — set endpoints (eyedroppers pick nodes in the
  viewport); choose the weight to **Minimize** (length, travel time, or an
  elevation-impedance with an `alpha`).
- **Shortest Path** — **Calculate Path** reports distance, node/edge counts, and
  travel time. **Summarize** and **Elevation Profile** add route rise and mean
  absolute grade.
- **K Alternative Routes** — compute the `K` shortest alternatives.
- **Batch (Many-to-Many)** — run routing over random OD pairs across CPU cores;
  reports the reached fraction and mean distance.

## Accessibility {#accessibility}

- **Isochrones** — reachable areas from a centre node for a set of trip times
  and travel speed; choose the isochrone mode (e.g. buffer-union with a buffer).
- **Ego Subgraph** — extract the neighbourhood subgraph around a node.
- **Network-Constrained DBSCAN** — cluster nodes by network (not Euclidean)
  distance.

## Statistics & Centrality {#statistics}

- **Basic Stats** — set/estimate the network area, then compute counts, total
  and average edge length, average degree, circuity, intersection and dead-end
  counts, and densities per km².
- **Circuity** — compute the average circuity.
- **Centrality** — choose a kind (e.g. betweenness/closeness), optionally
  weighted and/or using a fast approximation, and compute it.
- **Orientation Analysis** — orientation entropy, bearing distribution, and a
  rendered **Orientation Rose** image (configurable bin count).
- **Node-Pair Distances** — pick two nodes and compute straight-line, network,
  and travel-time distances plus circuity.

## Features & POIs {#features}

- **Download OSM Features** — fetch features (POIs, buildings, etc.) by place,
  address, point, bbox, polygon, or from an XML file.
- **Snap POIs to Street Network** — snap downloaded POIs to the nearest network
  element using a chosen mode.
- **Geocoding & Geometry** — geocode addresses, convert geocodes to
  GeoDataFrames, derive bounding boxes from points, convert bboxes to polygons,
  buffer geometry, interpolate points, and sample points on the graph.

## Elevation & Terrain {#elevation}

A staged pipeline for 3D terrain integration.

1. **Get Elevation Data** — choose a DEM source (OpenTopography with API key and
   dataset, Open-Elevation / OpenTopoData providers with resolution and parallel
   workers, or a local GeoTIFF). Choose whether to apply elevation to nodes
   and/or create a terrain mesh (Displace with subdivisions, or a subsampled
   grid). Set vertical scale and base offset. An **Export AOI** helper lets you
   fetch a DEM externally (Copernicus, EarthData) and re-enter it as a local
   GeoTIFF.
2. **3D Positions** — apply elevation to node positions (**Apply 3D Positions**)
   or **Flatten to 2D**.
3. **Slope / Grade** — compute edge grades; reports mean/max grade and the
   percentage of steep edges (>5%).
4. **Terrain Object** — show/hide or remove the generated terrain mesh.
5. **Basemap Texture** — drape a basemap onto the terrain from OSM/ESRI tiles,
   WMS, Mapbox, or MapTiler (API keys set in preferences); a live tile-count
   estimate warns before large downloads.

This subpanel powers the [urban-terrain tutorial](../../tutorials/03-urban-terrain.qmd).

## IO / Export {#io}

- **GraphML (OSMnx native)** — save/load the round-trip GraphML.
- **Export Formats (GIS interop)** — export to a chosen GIS format (GeoPackage,
  OSM XML, Gephi, SVG, etc.).
- **Graph Cache** — save the current network to the local cache and manage
  cached graphs.

## Typical workflow

1. **Download** a network by place, point, or bbox.
2. **Project to CRS** in **Graph Operations**, then add **Lengths**,
   **Speeds**, and **Travel Times** in **Network Attributes**.
3. Use **Routing**, **Accessibility**, and **Statistics & Centrality** for
   analysis, mapping results to attributes.
4. Add **Elevation & Terrain** for 3D context and a basemap, then visualize and
   render via the [SciGraphs Visualization panel](../scigraphs/visualization.qmd).
